text: This is 6 Minute English from BBC Learning English.
zh: 这里是BBC英语教学的《六分钟英语》节目。

text: I'm Neil.
zh: 我是尼尔。

text: And I'm Bekka.
zh: 我是贝卡。

text: Neil, I noticed that you were checking your phone before you came into the studio to record this programme.
zh: 尼尔,我注意到你进录音室录这期节目之前一直在看手机。

text: Yes, I probably was.
zh: 是的,可能确实是这样。

text: You know, Bekka, I spend far too much time looking at my phone and I really don't like it.
zh: 你知道吗,贝卡,我花在看手机上的时间实在太多了,我自己也不太喜欢这样。

text: How about you?
zh: 你呢?

text: I don't think you're alone.
zh: 我觉得不止你一个人这样。

text: I also spend way too much time looking at my phone.
zh: 我也花了太多时间盯着手机看。

text: Well, Bekka, we are both adults, but today we're talking about screen time and children.
zh: 好吧,贝卡,我们俩都是成年人,不过今天我们要聊的是屏幕使用时间和孩子的问题。

text: Screen time – that's how much time you spend using devices like smartphones, tablets and laptops.
zh: 屏幕使用时间,指的是你使用智能手机、平板电脑和笔记本电脑等设备所花的时间。

text: Yes, and it's a particular worry of many parents that their children spend too much time on these devices.
zh: 是的,很多家长都特别担心孩子在这些设备上花的时间太多。

text: At the end of last year, Australia banned access to social media apps for under -16s, and countries like the UK are considering similar measures.
zh: 去年年底,澳大利亚禁止16岁以下青少年使用社交媒体应用程序,英国等国家也在考虑采取类似措施。

text: So, why is this?
zh: 那么,这是为什么呢?

text: In this episode, we'll hear from a clinical psychologist and research associate at the University of Cambridge talking about screen time and children.
zh: 在这一期节目中,我们将听到剑桥大学的一位临床心理学家兼研究员谈论屏幕时间与儿童的话题。

text: As usual, we'll be learning some useful new words and phrases, and remember, you'll find all this episode's vocabulary along with the transcript on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 像往常一样,我们会学到一些实用的新单词和短语,记得可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到本期的词汇表和文字稿。

text: But first, Neil, I have a question for you.
zh: 不过首先,尼尔,我有个问题要问你。

text: According to the UK media regulator, how many children aged 3 to 4 have a smartphone?
zh: 根据英国媒体监管机构的数据,3到4岁的孩子中有多少比例拥有智能手机?

text: Is it a 1 in 20, b 1 in 10, or c 1 in 5?
zh: 是a. 二十分之一,b. 十分之一,还是c. 五分之一?

text: Wow!
zh: 哇!

text: Well, I'm going to guess 1 in 20, but I think it's probably more than that.
zh: 我猜是二十分之一,不过我觉得实际比例可能更高。

text: Well, we'll find out the answer later.
zh: 好,我们稍后揭晓答案。

text: Dr. Emily Gudeka is a research associate at the University of Cambridge.
zh: Emily Gudeka博士是剑桥大学的一名研究员。

text: Here she tells Shona McCullum, presenter of BBC World Service programme TechLife, her thoughts on whether we should take away children's screen devices completely.
zh: 她在这里向BBC世界服务节目《TechLife》的主持人Shona McCullum,谈了她对是否应该完全禁止孩子使用电子屏幕设备的看法。

text: Yeah, I think it's not as much as getting them away from technology, but being really intentional about how we use technology with children.
zh: 是的,我觉得重点不在于让孩子完全远离科技产品,而在于我们要有意识地思考如何和孩子一起使用科技。

text: For me, the bar absolutely needs to be higher.
zh: 对我来说,标准绝对需要提高。

text: and therefore I think about how to design products that think about childhood first
zh: 因此我会思考如何设计出以儿童为先的产品,

text: and really think about how children at different stages learn and develop and then see if there's a way that technology can enable that.
zh: 认真考虑孩子在不同阶段是如何学习和发展的,然后看看科技能否以某种方式促成这一点。

text: Emily highlights how rather than stopping children from using technology, parents need to be more intentional about how their children use it.
zh: Emily强调,与其阻止孩子使用科技产品,家长更需要有意识地引导孩子如何使用它。

text: An intention is a plan or reason to do something.
zh: intention(意图)指做某事的计划或理由。

text: So, to be intentional is to act with that plan or reason in mind.
zh: 所以,intentional(有意的)就是带着这种计划或理由去行动。

text: It may make you act more carefully as a result.
zh: 这可能会让你因此表现得更加谨慎。

text: Emily also says the bar needs to be higher.
zh: Emily还提到标准需要提高。

text: The idiom the bar is low means that the standards, expectations or requirements are minimal and very easy to meet.
zh: 习语the bar is low(标准很低)意思是标准、期望或要求非常宽松,很容易达到。

text: Therefore, she believes the bar needs to be higher.
zh: 因此,她认为标准需要提高。

text: Parents need to make their expectations higher.
zh: 家长需要提高自己的期望。

text: They need to think or act more carefully when considering how much time children spend with technology.
zh: 在考虑孩子花多少时间使用科技产品时,他们需要更谨慎地思考和行动。

text: You might also hear people say, set the bar higher or lower.
zh: 你也可能听到人们说,把标准set the bar higher or lower(提高或降低)。

text: Emily believes that being more intentional and setting the bar higher will enable children to learn and develop alongside technology.
zh: Emily认为,更有意识地行动并提高标准,将有助于孩子在科技的陪伴下学习和成长。

text: If you enable something, you encourage somebody's ability to do something or to make something possible.
zh: 如果你enable(促成)某件事,就是在帮助某人具备做某事的能力,或者让某事成为可能。

text: Now let's hear Dr Becky Kennedy, a clinical psychologist and founder of an online parenting platform,
zh: 接下来我们来听听临床心理学家、某在线育儿平台创始人Becky Kennedy博士的看法,

text: talking about how parents feel about tech and children for BBC World Service programme TechLife.
zh: 她在BBC世界服务节目《TechLife》中谈到了家长对科技与儿童问题的感受。

text: I find parents are very, very eager to understand what's really happening around technology in kids and then have practical,
zh: 我发现家长们都非常、非常渴望了解孩子身边科技产品的真实情况,

text: realistic things they can do in their home to make little shifts that feel better.
zh: 并希望得到一些切实可行的方法,在家里做出一些让他们感觉更安心的小小改变。

text: Becky has found that many parents are eager to understand their children's use of technology.
zh: Becky发现,很多家长都渴望了解孩子使用科技产品的情况。

text: Eager is an adjective that describes wanting to do or have something very much.
zh: eager是一个形容词,形容非常想做或想拥有某件事物。

text: Becky also talks about making little shifts.
zh: Becky还谈到了making little shifts(做一些小小的改变)。

text: A shift is a small change.
zh: shift指的是一个小的改变。

text: It can be used as a verb or a noun.
zh: 它既可以用作动词,也可以用作名词。

text: Becky shares an example of a shift that can help parents reduce children's screen time, moving the device somewhere it can't be seen.
zh: Becky举了一个例子,说明哪种小改变能帮助家长减少孩子的屏幕使用时间——把设备放到孩子看不到的地方。

text: You can even say to a kid, you know why I moved it?
zh: 你甚至可以对孩子说,你知道我为什么把它挪走吗?

text: It's actually because it's a really hard thing for me to expect from you, to see a screen in a room and not want to use it.
zh: 其实是因为,让你看着一个屏幕却不去用它,这对你来说太难了,我不该这样要求你。

text: I wasn't setting you up for success.
zh: 我这样做并没有为你创造成功的条件。

text: Becky explains how it's difficult for children to be expected to reduce their screen time if they can see their device.
zh: Becky解释说,如果孩子能看到自己的设备,却被要求减少使用屏幕的时间,这对他们来说是很难做到的。

text: Having the device in view doesn't set them up for success.
zh: 让设备一直处在视线范围内,并不利于孩子取得成功。

text: If you set someone up for something, you prepare them for it.
zh: 如果你set someone up for something(为某人创造条件),就是在为他们做准备。

text: Yes.
zh: 是的。

text: For example, earlier I set Neil up for a shock when I asked him a question about screen time.
zh: 比如说,刚才我问尼尔关于屏幕使用时间的问题时,就让他大吃一惊,没有准备。

text: Neil, I asked you, according to the UK media regulator, how many children aged 3 to 4 have a smartphone?
zh: 尼尔,我问你,根据英国媒体监管机构的数据,3到4岁的孩子中有多少比例拥有智能手机?

text: And I said 1 in 20.
zh: 我当时说是二十分之一。

text: Well, it is in fact, see, 1 in 5.
zh: 其实,正确答案是五分之一。

text: Wow, I thought it might be more than I said.
zh: 哇,我当时就觉得可能比我说的比例更高。

text: Now let's recap the vocabulary we've learned, starting with intentional.
zh: 现在我们来回顾一下学过的词汇,先从intentional开始。

text: It's an adjective that describes acting with a plan or reason in mind.
zh: 这是一个形容词,形容带着计划或理由去行动。

text: We had the expression, the bar needs to be higher.
zh: 我们还学了一个表达,the bar needs to be higher(标准需要提高)。

text: To set the bar higher is to make your expectations higher.
zh: set the bar higher就是提高你的期望。

text: We also had enable.
zh: 我们还学了enable(促成)这个词。

text: This is to encourage someone's ability to do something or to make something possible.
zh: 它指的是帮助某人具备做某事的能力,或者让某事成为可能。

text: To be eager is to want to do or have something very much.
zh: eager指非常想做或想拥有某件事物。

text: A shift is a small change.
zh: shift指小小的改变。

text: You can also use it as a verb to mean changing something slightly.
zh: 你也可以把它当动词用,表示稍微改变一下某件事。

text: And if you set someone up for something, you prepare them for it.
zh: 如果你set someone up for something,就是为他们做好准备。

text: Once again, our six minutes are up.
zh: 我们的六分钟又到时间了。

text: If you enjoyed this episode, you'll find a quiz and a free worksheet to practice the vocabulary you've learnt on our website bbclearningenglish .com.
zh: 如果你喜欢这期节目,可以在我们的网站bbclearningenglish.com上找到小测验和免费练习资料,巩固你学到的词汇。

text: See you again soon, but for now, it's goodbye.
zh: 我们下次再见,现在先说再见啦。

text: Six Minute English, from bbclearningenglish .com
zh: 《六分钟英语》,来自bbclearningenglish.com
